title: Pecteilis ochroleuca
intro: Pecteilis ochroleuca, also known as the golden yellow lady's slipper orchid, is a type of orchid native to East Asia, characterized by its unique, slipper-shaped flowers and delicate foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Pecteilis ochroleuca include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into herbal capsules and supplements.
taxonomy: Pecteilis ochroleuca is classified as a species of orchid within the family Orchidaceae. This genus, Pecteilis, is a small group of orchids native to Asia. The classification of Pecteilis ochroleuca reflects its unique characteristics and distinct features compared to other orchids. The species is also known as the yellowish green orchid due to its distinctive coloration. Within the Orchidaceae family, Pecteilis ochroleuca is classified as a member of the subfamily Orchidoideae.
